48:             if table
49:               say format_table_detail(connection, table)
50:             else
51:               say format_table_list(connection)
52:             end
53:           end
54:         end
55: 
56:         def run_models
57:           Rails.application.eager_load!
58: 
59:           models = ActiveRecord::Base.descendants
60:             .reject(&:abstract_class?)
61:             .select { |model| model.table_name.present? }
62:             .sort_by(&:name)
63: 
64:           data = models.map do |model|
65:             {
66:               model: model.name,
67:               table_name: model.table_name,
68:               associations: format_associations(model)
69:             }
70:           end
71: 
72:           say JSON.generate(data)
73:         end
74: 
75:         def run_explain(expression = nil)
76:           expression = resolve_expression(expression)
77: 
78:           if options[:sql]
79:             with_readonly_connection do |connection|
80:               result = connection.select_all("EXPLAIN #{expression}")
81:               say format_result(columns: result.columns, rows: result.rows, sql: "EXPLAIN #{expression}")
82:             end
83:           else
84:             relation = with_readonly_connection { eval(expression, TOPLEVEL_BINDING, "(query)", 1) }
85:             sql = relation.to_sql
86:             with_readonly_connection_for(relation.model.connection_class_for_self) do |connection|
87:               result = connection.select_all("EXPLAIN #{sql}")
88:               say format_result(columns: result.columns, rows: result.rows, sql: "EXPLAIN #{sql}")
89:             end
90:           end
91:         end
92: 
93:         def run_query(expression)
94:           expression = resolve_expression(expression)
95:           page = [ options[:page], 1 ].max
96:           per = [ [ options[:per], 1 ].max, 10_000 ].min
97: 
98:           start_time = Process.clock_gettime(Process::CLOCK_MONOTONIC)
99: 
100:           result = if options[:sql]
101:             with_readonly_connection do |connection|
102:               execute_sql(connection: connection, sql: expression, page: page, per: per)
103:             end
104:           else
105:             execute_expression(expression: expression, page: page, per: per)
106:           end
107: 
108:           elapsed_ms = ((Process.clock_gettime(Process::CLOCK_MONOTONIC) - start_time) * 1000).round(1)
109: 
110:           say format_result(**result, elapsed_ms: elapsed_ms, page: page, per: per)
111:         end
112: 
113:         def with_readonly_connection(&block)
114:           with_readonly_connection_for(ActiveRecord::Base, &block)
115:         end
116: 
117:         def with_readonly_connection_for(connection_class, &block)
118:           if options[:database]
119:             with_explicit_database(options[:database], &block)
120:           elsif reading_role_available?(connection_class)
121:             connection_class.connected_to(role: :reading) do
122:               connection_class.with_connection(&block)
123:             end
124:           else
125:             connection_class.while_preventing_writes do
126:               connection_class.with_connection(&block)
127:             end
128:           end
129:         end
130: 
131:         def with_explicit_database(database)
132:           original = ActiveRecord::Base.connection_db_config
133:           begin
134:             ActiveRecord::Base.establish_connection(database.to_sym)
135:             ActiveRecord::Base.while_preventing_writes do
136:               yield ActiveRecord::Base.lease_connection
137:             end
138:           ensure
139:             ActiveRecord::Base.establish_connection(original)
140:           end
141:         end
142: 
143:         def reading_role_available?(connection_class)
144:           connection_class.connected_to(role: :reading) do
145:             connection_class.lease_connection
146:           end
147:           true
148:         rescue ActiveRecord::ConnectionNotEstablished
149:           false
150:         end
151: 
152:         def execute_sql(connection:, sql:, page:, per:)
153:           unless sql.gsub(/--.*$|\/\*.*?\*\//m, "").match?(/\bLIMIT\b/i)
154:             offset = (page - 1) * per
155:             sql = "#{sql.rstrip.chomp(';')} LIMIT #{per + 1}"
156:             sql += " OFFSET #{offset}" if offset > 0
157:           end
158: 
159:           active_record_result = connection.select_all(sql)
160:           rows = active_record_result.rows
161: 
162:           tabular_result(columns: active_record_result.columns, rows: rows.first(per), sql: sql, truncated: rows.length > per)
163:         end
164: 
165:         def execute_expression(expression:, page:, per:)
166:           result = with_readonly_connection { eval(expression, TOPLEVEL_BINDING, "(query)", 1) }
167:           columns, rows, sql, truncated = tabular_result_parts_for(result, expression: expression, page: page, per: per)
168: 
169:           tabular_result(columns: columns, rows: rows, sql: sql, truncated: truncated)
170:         end
171: 
172:         def tabular_result_parts_for(result, expression:, page:, per:)
173:           case result
174:           when ActiveRecord::Relation
175:             relation = result.offset((page - 1) * per).limit(per + 1)
176:             relation_sql = relation.to_sql
177: 
178:             with_readonly_connection_for(relation.model.connection_class_for_self) do |connection|
179:               active_record_result = connection.select_all(relation_sql)
180:               rows = active_record_result.rows
181: 
182:               [ active_record_result.columns, rows.first(per), relation_sql, rows.length > per ]
183:             end
184:           when ActiveRecord::Result
185:             [ result.columns, result.rows, expression, false ]
186:           when ActiveRecord::Base
187:             attributes = result.attributes
188: 
189:             [ attributes.keys, [ attributes.values ], expression, false ]
190:           when Hash
191:             [ [ "key", "value" ], result.map { |key, val| [ key, val ] }, expression, false ]
192:           when Array
193:             peek_on_result = result.first
194: 
195:             if peek_on_result.is_a?(ActiveRecord::Base)
196:               columns = peek_on_result.attribute_names
197:               rows = result.map { |record| record.attributes.values }
198:             else
199:               rows = result.map { |value| Array(value) }
200:               columns = Array.new(rows.first&.length.to_i) { |index| "column_#{index}" }
201:             end
202: 
203:             [ columns, rows, expression, false ]
204:           else
205:             [ [ "result" ], [ [ result ] ], expression, false ]
206:           end
207:         end
208: 
209:         def tabular_result(columns:, rows:, sql:, truncated: false)
210:           { columns: columns, rows: rows, sql: sql, truncated: truncated }
211:         end
212: 
213:         def format_result(columns:, rows:, sql:, elapsed_ms: 0, page: 1, per: rows.length, truncated: false)
214:           JSON.generate({
215:             columns: columns,
216:             rows: rows,
217:             meta: {
218:               row_count: rows.length,
219:               query_time_ms: elapsed_ms,
220:               page: page,
221:               per_page: per,
222:               has_more: truncated,
223:               sql: sql
224:             }
225:           })
226:         end
227: 
228:         def format_table_list(connection)
229:           tables = connection.tables.sort
230:           rows = tables.map { |table| [ table ] }
231: 
232:           format_result(columns: [ "table_name" ], rows: rows, sql: "")
233:         end
234: 
235:         def format_table_detail(connection, table)
236:           raise ArgumentError, "Table '#{table}' does not exist" unless connection.table_exists?(table)
237: 
238:           columns = connection.columns(table)
239:           indexes = connection.indexes(table)
240:           model = model_for_table(table)
241: 
242:           JSON.generate({
243:             table: table,
244:             columns: columns.map do |col|
245:               { name: col.name, type: col.sql_type, null: col.null, default: col.default }
246:             end,
247:             indexes: indexes.map do |idx|
248:               { name: idx.name, columns: idx.columns, unique: idx.unique }

44:   # Convert an enumerable to a hash, using the block result as the key and the

52:   def index_by
53:     if block_given?
54:       result = {}
55:       each { |elem| result[yield(elem)] = elem }
56:       result

62:   # Convert an enumerable to a hash, using the element as the key and the block

67:   #   %i( title body ).index_with { |attr_name| post.public_send(attr_name) }
68:   #   # => { title: "hey there", body: "what's up?" }
69:   #
70:   # If an argument is passed instead of a block, it will be used as the value

75:   def index_with(default = (no_default = true))
76:     if block_given?

79:       result

89:   # Returns +true+ if the enumerable has more than 1 element. Functionally

92:   # if more than one person is over 26.

97:         cnt += 1 if yield(*args)

138:   # Extract the given key from each element in the enumerable.

145:   def pluck(*keys)
146:     if keys.many?

175:   #   # =>  [1, 2, true]
176:   #

195:   # If the Enumerable has additional elements that aren't named in the +series+, these are not included in the result, unless
196:   # the +filter+ option is set to +false+.

200:     else

205:   # Returns the sole item in the enumerable. If there are no items, or more
206:   # than one item, raises Enumerable::SoleItemExpectedError.
207:   #
208:   #   ["x"].sole          # => "x"

210:   #   { a: 1, b: 2 }.sole # => Enumerable::SoleItemExpectedError: multiple items found
211:   def sole
212:     result = nil
213:     found = false
214: 
215:     each do |*element|
216:       if found
217:         raise SoleItemExpectedError, "multiple items found"
218:       end
219: 
220:       result = element.size == 1 ? element[0] : element
221:       found = true
222:     end
223: 
224:     if found
225:       result
226:     else
227:       raise SoleItemExpectedError, "no item found"
228:     end
229:   end
230: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module ActiveRecord
4:   module Sanitization
5:     extend ActiveSupport::Concern
6: 
7:     module ClassMethods
8:       # Accepts an array of SQL conditions and sanitizes them into a valid
9:       # SQL fragment for a WHERE clause.
10:       #
11:       #   sanitize_sql_for_conditions(["name=? and group_id=?", "foo'bar", 4])
12:       #   # => "name='foo''bar' and group_id=4"
13:       #
14:       #   sanitize_sql_for_conditions(["name=:name and group_id=:group_id", name: "foo'bar", group_id: 4])
15:       #   # => "name='foo''bar' and group_id='4'"
16:       #
17:       #   sanitize_sql_for_conditions(["name='%s' and group_id='%s'", "foo'bar", 4])
18:       #   # => "name='foo''bar' and group_id='4'"
19:       #
20:       # This method will NOT sanitize an SQL string since it won't contain
21:       # any conditions in it and will return the string as is.
22:       #
23:       #   sanitize_sql_for_conditions("name='foo''bar' and group_id='4'")
24:       #   # => "name='foo''bar' and group_id='4'"
25:       #
26:       # Note that this sanitization method is not schema-aware, hence won't do any type casting
27:       # and will directly use the database adapter's +quote+ method.
28:       # For MySQL specifically this means that numeric parameters will be quoted as strings
29:       # to prevent query manipulation attacks.
30:       #
31:       #   sanitize_sql_for_conditions(["role = ?", 0])
32:       #   # => "role = '0'"
33:       def sanitize_sql_for_conditions(condition)
34:         return nil if condition.blank?
35: 
36:         case condition
37:         when Array; sanitize_sql_array(condition)
38:         else        condition
39:         end
40:       end
41:       alias :sanitize_sql :sanitize_sql_for_conditions
42: 
43:       # Accepts an array or hash of SQL conditions and sanitizes them into
44:       # a valid SQL fragment for a SET clause.
45:       #
46:       #   sanitize_sql_for_assignment(["name=? and group_id=?", nil, 4])
47:       #   # => "name=NULL and group_id=4"
48:       #
49:       #   sanitize_sql_for_assignment(["name=:name and group_id=:group_id", name: nil, group_id: 4])
50:       #   # => "name=NULL and group_id=4"
51:       #
52:       #   Post.sanitize_sql_for_assignment({ name: nil, group_id: 4 })
53:       #   # => "`posts`.`name` = NULL, `posts`.`group_id` = 4"
54:       #
55:       # This method will NOT sanitize an SQL string since it won't contain
56:       # any conditions in it and will return the string as is.
57:       #
58:       #   sanitize_sql_for_assignment("name=NULL and group_id='4'")
59:       #   # => "name=NULL and group_id='4'"
60:       #
61:       # Note that this sanitization method is not schema-aware, hence won't do any type casting
62:       # and will directly use the database adapter's +quote+ method.
63:       # For MySQL specifically this means that numeric parameters will be quoted as strings
64:       # to prevent query manipulation attacks.
65:       #
66:       #   sanitize_sql_for_assignment(["role = ?", 0])
67:       #   # => "role = '0'"
68:       def sanitize_sql_for_assignment(assignments, default_table_name = table_name)
69:         case assignments
70:         when Array; sanitize_sql_array(assignments)
71:         when Hash;  sanitize_sql_hash_for_assignment(assignments, default_table_name)
72:         else        assignments
73:         end
74:       end
75: 
76:       # Accepts an array, or string of SQL conditions and sanitizes
77:       # them into a valid SQL fragment for an ORDER clause.
78:       #
79:       #   sanitize_sql_for_order([Arel.sql("field(id, ?)"), [1,3,2]])
80:       #   # => "field(id, 1,3,2)"
81:       #
82:       #   sanitize_sql_for_order("id ASC")
83:       #   # => "id ASC"
84:       def sanitize_sql_for_order(condition)
85:         if condition.is_a?(Array) && condition.first.to_s.include?("?")
86:           disallow_raw_sql!(
87:             [condition.first],
88:             permit: adapter_class.column_name_with_order_matcher
89:           )
90: 
91:           # Ensure we aren't dealing with a subclass of String that might
92:           # override methods we use (e.g. Arel::Nodes::SqlLiteral).
93:           if condition.first.kind_of?(String) && !condition.first.instance_of?(String)
94:             condition = [String.new(condition.first), *condition[1..-1]]
95:           end
96: 
97:           Arel.sql(sanitize_sql_array(condition))
98:         else
99:           condition
100:         end
101:       end
102: 
103:       # Sanitizes a hash of attribute/value pairs into SQL conditions for a SET clause.
104:       #
105:       #   sanitize_sql_hash_for_assignment({ status: nil, group_id: 1 }, "posts")
106:       #   # => "`posts`.`status` = NULL, `posts`.`group_id` = 1"
107:       def sanitize_sql_hash_for_assignment(attrs, table)
108:         with_connection do |c|
109:           attrs.map do |attr, value|
110:             type = type_for_attribute(attr)
111:             value = type.serialize(type.cast(value))
112:             "#{c.quote_table_name_for_assignment(table, attr)} = #{c.quote(value)}"
113:           end.join(", ")
114:         end
115:       end
116: 
117:       # Sanitizes a +string+ so that it is safe to use within an SQL
118:       # LIKE statement. This method uses +escape_character+ to escape all
119:       # occurrences of itself, "_" and "%".
120:       #
121:       #   sanitize_sql_like("100% true!")
122:       #   # => "100\\% true!"
123:       #
124:       #   sanitize_sql_like("snake_cased_string")
125:       #   # => "snake\\_cased\\_string"
126:       #
127:       #   sanitize_sql_like("100% true!", "!")
128:       #   # => "100!% true!!"
129:       #
130:       #   sanitize_sql_like("snake_cased_string", "!")
131:       #   # => "snake!_cased!_string"
132:       def sanitize_sql_like(string, escape_character = "\\")
133:         if string.include?(escape_character) && escape_character != "%" && escape_character != "_"
134:           string = string.gsub(escape_character, '\0\0')
135:         end
136: 
137:         string.gsub(/(?=[%_])/, escape_character)
138:       end
139: 
140:       # Accepts an array of conditions. The array has each value
141:       # sanitized and interpolated into the SQL statement. If using named bind
142:       # variables in SQL statements where a colon is required verbatim use a
143:       # backslash to escape.
144:       #
145:       #   sanitize_sql_array(["name=? and group_id=?", "foo'bar", 4])
146:       #   # => "name='foo''bar' and group_id=4"
147:       #
148:       #   sanitize_sql_array(["name=:name and group_id=:group_id", name: "foo'bar", group_id: 4])
149:       #   # => "name='foo''bar' and group_id=4"
150:       #
151:       #   sanitize_sql_array(["TO_TIMESTAMP(:date, 'YYYY/MM/DD HH12\\:MI\\:SS')", date: "foo"])
152:       #   # => "TO_TIMESTAMP('foo', 'YYYY/MM/DD HH12:MI:SS')"
153:       #
154:       #   sanitize_sql_array(["name='%s' and group_id='%s'", "foo'bar", 4])
155:       #   # => "name='foo''bar' and group_id='4'"
156:       #
157:       # Note that this sanitization method is not schema-aware, hence won't do any type casting
158:       # and will directly use the database adapter's +quote+ method.
159:       # For MySQL specifically this means that numeric parameters will be quoted as strings
160:       # to prevent query manipulation attacks.
161:       #
162:       #   sanitize_sql_array(["role = ?", 0])
163:       #   # => "role = '0'"
164:       #
165:       # Before using this method, please consider if Arel.sql would be better for your use-case
166:       def sanitize_sql_array(ary)
167:         statement, *values = ary
168:         if values.first.is_a?(Hash) && /:\w+/.match?(statement)
169:           with_connection do |c|
170:             replace_named_bind_variables(c, statement, values.first)
171:           end
172:         elsif statement.include?("?")
173:           with_connection do |c|
174:             replace_bind_variables(c, statement, values)
175:           end
176:         elsif statement.blank?
177:           statement
178:         else
179:           with_connection do |c|
180:             statement % values.collect { |value| c.quote_string(value.to_s) }
181:           end
182:         end
183:       end
184: 
185:       def disallow_raw_sql!(args, permit: adapter_class.column_name_matcher) # :nodoc:
186:         unexpected = nil
187:         args.each do |arg|
188:           next if arg.is_a?(Symbol) || Arel.arel_node?(arg) || permit.match?(arg.to_s.strip)
189:           (unexpected ||= []) << arg
190:         end
191: 
192:         if unexpected
193:           raise(ActiveRecord::UnknownAttributeReference,
194:             "Dangerous query method (method whose arguments are used as raw " \
195:             "SQL) called with non-attribute argument(s): " \
196:             "#{unexpected.map(&:inspect).join(", ")}." \
197:             "This method should not be called with user-provided values, such as request " \
198:             "parameters or model attributes. Known-safe values can be passed " \
199:             "by wrapping them in Arel.sql()."
200:           )
201:         end
202:       end
203: 
204:       private
205:         def replace_bind_variables(connection, statement, values)
206:           raise_if_bind_arity_mismatch(statement, statement.count("?"), values.size)
207:           bound = values.dup
208:           statement.gsub(/\?/) do
209:             replace_bind_variable(connection, bound.shift)
210:           end
211:         end
212: 
213:         def replace_bind_variable(connection, value)
214:           if ActiveRecord::Relation === value
215:             value.to_sql
216:           else
217:             quote_bound_value(connection, value)
218:           end
219:         end
220: 
221:         def replace_named_bind_variables(connection, statement, bind_vars)
222:           statement.gsub(/([:\\]?):([a-zA-Z]\w*)/) do |match|
223:             if $1 == ":" # skip PostgreSQL casts
224:               match # return the whole match
225:             elsif $1 == "\\" # escaped literal colon
226:               match[1..-1] # return match with escaping backlash char removed
227:             elsif bind_vars.include?(match = $2.to_sym)
228:               replace_bind_variable(connection, bind_vars[match])
229:             else
230:               raise PreparedStatementInvalid, "missing value for :#{match} in #{statement}"
231:             end
232:           end
233:         end
234: 
235:         def quote_bound_value(connection, value)
236:           if value.respond_to?(:map) && !value.acts_like?(:string)
237:             values = value.map { |v| v.respond_to?(:id_for_database) ? v.id_for_database : v }
238:             if values.empty?
239:               connection.quote(connection.cast_bound_value(nil))
240:             else
241:               values.map! { |v| connection.quote(connection.cast_bound_value(v)) }.join(",")
242:             end
243:           else
244:             value = value.id_for_database if value.respond_to?(:id_for_database)
245:             connection.quote(connection.cast_bound_value(value))
246:           end
247:         end

21:       # Applying values to a relation has some side effects. E.g.
22:       # interpolation might take place for where values. So we should
23:       # build a relation to merge in rather than directly merging
24:       # the values.
25:       def other
26:         other = Relation.create(
27:           relation.model,
28:           table: relation.table,
29:           predicate_builder: relation.predicate_builder
30:         )
31:         hash.each do |k, v|
32:           k = :_select if k == :select
33:           if Array === v
34:             other.public_send("#{k}!", *v)
35:           else
36:             other.public_send("#{k}!", v)
37:           end
38:         end
39:         other
40:       end
41:     end
42: 
43:     class Merger # :nodoc:
44:       attr_reader :relation, :values, :other
45: 
46:       def initialize(relation, other)
47:         @relation = relation
48:         @values   = other.values
49:         @other    = other
50:       end
51: 
52:       NORMAL_VALUES = (Relation::VALUE_METHODS - Relation::CLAUSE_METHODS -
53:                       [
54:                         :select, :includes, :preload, :joins, :left_outer_joins,
55:                         :order, :reverse_order, :lock, :create_with, :reordering
56:                       ]).freeze
57: 
58:       def merge
59:         NORMAL_VALUES.each do |name|
60:           value = values[name]
61:           # The unless clause is here mostly for performance reasons (since the `send` call might be moderately
62:           # expensive), most of the time the value is going to be `nil` or `.blank?`, the only catch is that
63:           # `false.blank?` returns `true`, so there needs to be an extra check so that explicit `false` values
64:           # don't fall through the cracks.
65:           unless value.nil? || (value.blank? && false != value)
66:             relation.public_send(:"#{name}!", *value)
67:           end
68:         end
69: 
70:         relation.none! if other.null_relation?
71: 
72:         merge_select_values
73:         merge_multi_values
74:         merge_single_values
75:         merge_clauses
76:         merge_preloads
77:         merge_joins
78:         merge_outer_joins
79: 
80:         relation
81:       end
82: 
83:       private
84:         def merge_select_values
85:           return if other.select_values.empty?
86: 
87:           if other.model == relation.model
88:             relation.select_values |= other.select_values
89:           else
90:             relation.select_values |= other.instance_eval do
91:               arel_columns(select_values)
92:             end
93:           end
94:         end
95: 
96:         def merge_preloads
97:           return if other.preload_values.empty? && other.includes_values.empty?
98: 
99:           if other.model == relation.model
100:             relation.preload_values |= other.preload_values unless other.preload_values.empty?
101:             relation.includes_values |= other.includes_values unless other.includes_values.empty?
102:           else
103:             reflection = relation.model.reflect_on_all_associations.find do |r|
104:               r.class_name == other.model.name
105:             end || return
106: 
107:             unless other.preload_values.empty?
108:               relation.preload! reflection.name => other.preload_values
109:             end
110: 
111:             unless other.includes_values.empty?
112:               relation.includes! reflection.name => other.includes_values
113:             end
114:           end
115:         end
116: 
117:         def merge_joins
118:           return if other.joins_values.empty?
119: 
120:           if other.model == relation.model
121:             relation.joins_values |= other.joins_values
122:           else
123:             associations, others = other.joins_values.partition do |join|
124:               case join
125:               when Hash, Symbol, Array; true
126:               end
127:             end
128: 
129:             join_dependency = other.construct_join_dependency(
130:               associations, Arel::Nodes::InnerJoin
131:             )
132:             relation.joins!(join_dependency, *others)
133:           end
134:         end
135: 
136:         def merge_outer_joins
137:           return if other.left_outer_joins_values.empty?
138: 
139:           if other.model == relation.model
140:             relation.left_outer_joins_values |= other.left_outer_joins_values
141:           else
142:             associations, others = other.left_outer_joins_values.partition do |join|
143:               case join
144:               when Hash, Symbol, Array; true
145:               end
146:             end
147: 
148:             join_dependency = other.construct_join_dependency(
149:               associations, Arel::Nodes::OuterJoin
150:             )
151:             relation.left_outer_joins!(join_dependency, *others)
152:           end
153:         end
154: 
155:         def merge_multi_values
156:           if other.reordering_value
157:             # override any order specified in the original relation
158:             relation.reorder!(*other.order_values)
159:           elsif other.order_values.any?
160:             # merge in order_values from relation
161:             relation.order!(*other.order_values)
162:           end
163: 
164:           relation.extending!(*other.extensions)
165:         end
166:

168:           relation.lock_value ||= other.lock_value if other.lock_value
169: 
170:           unless other.create_with_value.blank?
171:             relation.create_with_value = (relation.create_with_value || {}).merge(other.create_with_value)
172:           end
173:         end
174: 
175:         def merge_clauses
176:           relation.from_clause = other.from_clause if replace_from_clause?
177: 
178:           where_clause = relation.where_clause.merge(other.where_clause)
179:           relation.where_clause = where_clause unless where_clause.empty?
180: 
181:           having_clause = relation.having_clause.merge(other.having_clause)
182:           relation.having_clause = having_clause unless having_clause.empty?
183:         end
184: 
185:         def replace_from_clause?
186:           relation.from_clause.empty? && !other.from_clause.empty? &&

188:         end
